join_order.notes: Use the existing customer segment postings to enumerate BUILDING customers, then traverse orders_custkey_postings to set a compact qualifying-order-row bitset. Consult orders_orderdate_zone_map by order-row block first: skip whole blocks when mins >= 9204, accept whole blocks when maxs < 9204, and read o_orderdate only for fallback blocks.

join_order.build: qualifying_order_rows(bitset keyed by orders row id)
join_order.notes: Because lineitem_orderkey_groups aligns one group per orders row, scan groups sequentially in order-row order and touch lineitem payload columns only when the qualifying-order bitset is set. Fetch o_orderkey, o_orderdate, and o_shippriority sparsely for surviving rows only.

data_structures.dim_filter: bitset(15000000) qualifying order rows built from customer_mktsegment_postings -> c_custkey -> orders_custkey_postings with orders_orderdate_zone_map block states
data_structures.fact_join: pre_built_index(lineitem_orderkey_groups row-aligned to orders row ids)
data_structures.aggregation: array_direct(per-order scalar revenue accumulation with thread-local topk heaps)

indexes.purpose: enumerate only orders belonging to BUILDING customers instead of scanning all 15M orders
skip or fast-accept order-row blocks before any row-level o_orderdate load during bitset construction
scan row-aligned lineitem runs sequentially and aggregate only bitset-selected orders

optimization_notes: Root cause: iteration 1 still performs unnecessary full-table work on orders by reading o_custkey and usually o_orderdate for all 15M rows even though the existing postings indexes can identify a much smaller candidate set first; only about 300K BUILDING customers and about 1.48M orders survive the customer-plus-date predicates. Strategy category A (plan restructuring): switch to a hybrid indexed plan that traverses customer_mktsegment_postings and orders_custkey_postings to build a compact 15M-row qualifying-order bitset, using orders_orderdate_zone_map to avoid row-level date loads for whole blocks, then scan the row-aligned lineitem_orderkey_groups sequentially and touch order payload and lineitem payload columns only for bitset-selected rows. This keeps the compact selection structure cache-resident, removes the full orders metadata scan, and preserves sequential access for the expensive lineitem aggregation step.
